name: M.B.A. in Management of Technology

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The 23 referenced courses span every MBA functional area (ACCT 615, FIN 600/624, HRM 601, MRKT 620/631, MIS 645) and cluster convincingly around the technology-management theme (MGMT 620 Management of Technology, MRKT 632/636 tech marketing, HRM 630 technological change), so the content coheres well thematically. But with requirementGroups=0 and no parsed plan of study, there is no visible core-versus-elective structure, no foundation-to-capstone progression, and deepestPrereqChain is only 1, so a prospective student cannot see how the degree is sequenced.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish a plan of study that separates a required functional core (accounting, finance, OB, marketing, MIS, plus MGMT 620) from the MOT concentration electives and ends in an integrative strategy capstone.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: missingCourseCount is 0, so every referenced code resolves cleanly with no dangling prerequisites, and the shallow deepestPrereqChain of 1 (ACCT 640 after ACCT 615, FIN 624/641/650 after FIN 600, MIS 648 after MIS 645) is appropriate for a professional MBA where graduate electives are meant to be largely self-contained. The narrative prerequisite on FIN 611 (prior Python/Java/R programming) is reasonable rather than over-constraining.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a second-level prerequisite tying the analytics electives together (e.g., require MGMT 630 or MGMT 635 ahead of MIS 648) so the data track shows intentional sequencing.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives are present in the catalog data, so a prospective MBA student cannot see what managerial competencies the degree certifies beyond inferring them from course titles.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 4-6 MBA program learning outcomes covering technology strategy, data-driven decision making, financial acumen, and leadership of technical organizations.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is null and creditReconcileDelta is uncomputable with no parsed plan, so although every listed course is a uniform 3 credits, the overall degree total (an MBA is typically ~36-48 credits) and the split between core and concentration electives are not transparent.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total credit requirement and show how it divides among required core courses, the MOT concentration, and free electives.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Each of the 23 courses carries a clear, specific description and consistent 3-credit labeling, which reads well, but the entry lacks a plan of study, labeled terms, a credit summary, and any stated outcomes, so the page functions as a course list rather than a structured degree presentation.
dimensions.suggestion: Wrap the course list in a labeled core/elective plan of study with a credit-summary header so the page orients a prospective student at a glance.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The curriculum is notably current for a technology MBA: ACCT 640 Big Data Analytics for Accounting, FIN 611 FinTech, MGMT 635 Data Mining, MIS 648 Decision Support Systems, and MRKT 645 Digital Marketing Strategy all reflect contemporary data-driven practice, and MGMT 620/680 keep the innovation and entrepreneurship angle live.
dimensions.suggestion: Add an explicit AI-for-managers or generative-AI-in-business elective so the degree signals the current frontier rather than stopping at classical data mining and decision support.

descriptionQuality.justification: Descriptions are present and substantive across all 23 courses, each conveying specific scope (ACCT 640 on hands-on data-analytics tools, FIN 641 on option valuation and futures pricing, MRKT 632 on marketing in hyper-competitive tech markets) rather than placeholder text.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are stated unambiguously and reconcile cleanly (missingCourseCount=0): simple code prerequisites where they exist and a clear narrative programming expectation on FIN 611, with no dangling references or contradictory grade conditions.

missingTopics.rationale: The set has entrepreneurial strategy (MGMT 680) but no general strategic-management capstone integrating the functional core (ACCT/FIN/MRKT/MIS) into a single decision context, which most AACSB-style MBAs require.
No operations/process-management course appears among the 23, leaving a classic MBA core function uncovered for managers of technology-driven production and service delivery.
There is no microeconomics/managerial-economics foundation in the sample, so pricing, market structure, and demand analysis underpinning the finance and marketing electives are not established.
Legal/ethical and sustainability content (offered as MGMT 691 elsewhere in the department) does not surface here, a growing expectation for MBA accreditation and responsible management of technology.
Analytics stops at data mining (MGMT 635) and decision support (MIS 648); a modern MBA needs applied predictive modeling and data-visualization/storytelling for executive audiences.

emergingTrends.trend: Generative AI and LLM tooling for management
emergingTrends.rationale: MGMT 635 and MIS 648 should be modernized to cover generative-AI copilots for analysis, drafting, and decision support that managers now use daily.
FIN 611 already opens the FinTech door; deepening it toward payments, digital assets, and algorithmic finance would keep the finance track at the industry frontier.
MRKT 645 Digital Marketing Strategy can extend to programmatic advertising, marketing-mix modeling, and generative content, which now define digital marketing practice.
ESG reporting and responsible-AI governance are becoming board-level concerns a technology MBA should position graduates to lead.
MGMT 620 and MRKT 636 could incorporate agile product management and multi-sided platform strategy, which dominate how technology firms organize and compete.

topSuggestions: Publish a plan of study that distinguishes the required functional core from the MOT concentration electives and terminates in an integrative strategy capstone.
State the total credit requirement and the core/elective credit split to fix the null credit transparency.
Add the missing MBA core anchors: operations/supply-chain management, managerial economics, and a strategic-management capstone.
Publish 4-6 program-level learning outcomes describing the managerial and technology-leadership competencies the degree certifies.
Introduce an AI-for-managers / generative-AI-in-business elective and modernize MGMT 635 and MIS 648 to reflect current analytics practice.
